content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer CCC and an array AAA = A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã which has distinct positive integers. It is guaranteed that Ai‚â§CA_i \leq CAi‚Äã‚â§C for 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚â§i‚â§N. You have an infinite supply of coins of denomination AiA_iAi‚Äã for 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚â§i‚â§N. You have another array W=W1,W2,‚Ä¶WNW = W_1, W_2, \ldots W_NW=W1‚Äã,W2‚Äã,‚Ä¶WN‚Äã. This denotes that a single coin of denomination AiA_iAi‚Äã has a weight of WiW_iWi‚Äã.

You are given MMM queries: Q1,Q2,‚Ä¶,QMQ_1, Q_2, \ldots, Q_MQ1‚Äã,Q2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,QM‚Äã. You need to get a collection of coins, such that their values add up to exactly QiQ_iQi‚Äã (ie. sum of denominations), and the sum of their weights is minimized. Output this minimum weight, or say that it is impossible to get exactly a sum of QiQ_iQi‚Äã. As the minimum weight could be huge, output it module 109+710^9 +7109+7.

###Input:


The first line of the input contains three integers: NNN, CCC and MMM.
The i-th of the next NNN lines contains two numbers AiA_iAi‚Äã and WiW_iWi‚Äã, which have been described above.
The i-th of the next MMM lines contains a single integer: QiQ_iQi‚Äã.
###Output:


MMM lines, each of which should contain a single integer. If it is possible to achieve the queried value, then your output should be the remainder when the minimum total weight is divided by 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
If it is not possible to achieve the exact value in the query, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1. Note that we do not take modulo anything here.
###Constraints


1‚â§N‚â§C‚â§1001 \leq N \leq C \leq 1001‚â§N‚â§C‚â§100
1‚â§M‚â§1061 \leq M \leq 10^61‚â§M‚â§106
1‚â§Ai‚â§C1 \leq A_i \leq C1‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§C
Ai‚âÝAjA_i \neq A_jAi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=Aj‚Äã, if i‚âÝji \neq jiÓÄÝ=j
1‚â§Wi‚â§1091 \leq W_i \leq 10^91‚â§Wi‚Äã‚â§109
1‚â§Qi‚â§10181 \leq Q_i \leq 10^{18}1‚â§Qi‚Äã‚â§1018
###Subtasks


12 points : 1‚â§Qi‚â§1061 \leq Q_i \leq 10^61‚â§Qi‚Äã‚â§106
88 points : No further constraints.
###Sample Input:
3 100 2
2 50
8 1
3 40
9
7

###Sample Output:
120
140

###Explanation:
You can get a total value of 999 by taking three coins of denomination 333. Their total weight comes to 3‚àó40=1203 * 40 = 1203‚àó40=120. You can check that you cannot do better than this.

You can get a total value of 777 by taking two coins of denomination 222 and one coin of denomination 333 ((2‚àó2)+3=7(2 * 2) + 3 = 7(2‚àó2)+3=7). Their total weight comes to (2‚àó50)+40=140(2 * 50) + 40 = 140(2‚àó50)+40=140. You can check that you cannot do better than this.
